product.name: Ari Bez
product.familyDescriptorPl: Tkaniny i dzianiny z włókna AriPam odporne na UV
product.familyDescriptorEn: UV-resistant AriPam fibre fabrics and knits
product.categorySourceGroup: AriPam spun fabrics for shoe lining
product.specLine: 180 g/m², 100% AriPam, Uv protective, 1/1 Plain Woven Fabric
descriptions: It is used as a lining inside of the shoes.
Ari Bez can also be used on outdoor medical cloth.
180 g/m², 100% AriPam, Uv protective, 1/1 Plain Woven Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp > 1000N ; Weft > 800N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp > 25N ; Weft > 20N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Description mentions it 'can also be used on outdoor medical cloth', but this is a secondary, vague mention without specific medical garment context, standards, or barrier properties described, making it insufficiently direct for a confident match.

stage1.noMatchReason: This is a shoe-lining fabric (100% AriPam, UV resistant, plain weave) intended primarily as lining inside shoes, with a secondary vague mention of outdoor medical cloth use. No explicit ties to military, firefighting, energy, welding, motorcycle, hi-vis, sport, architecture-building, or transport applications are stated in the product data.

evidence: It is used as a lining inside of the shoes.
AriPam spun fabrics for shoe lining
primary.rationale: The product is explicitly and repeatedly described as a shoe lining fabric, with the technical parameters and category source group directly confirming footwear use as the dominant application.

evidence: Ari Bez can also be used on outdoor medical cloth.
secondary.rationale: A secondary explicit mention of outdoor medical cloth use provides independent, though vague, support for a medical application beyond the primary footwear use.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: UV resistance and outdoor mention are not tied to a specific outdoor garment use; footwear lining and vague medical use are more directly supported.
